Article Title: 'Put your own house in order': India slams Pakistan over crossborder terror at UN
Article Text:
India's Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Parvathaneni Harish (PTI file photo)

NEW DELHI: India on Wednesday once again hit out at Pakistan over its continued use of crossborder terrorism, accusing Islamabad of employing terrorism as a "tool of state policy". New Delhi also reiterated that Jammu and Kashmir "has always been, is, and will remain" an integral and inalienable part of India.The remarks were made by India's Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Ambassador Parvathaneni Harish, during the High-Level Open Debate of the UN Security Council on "Natural Resources Governance: the Foundation of Peace, Security and Prosperity."Responding to Pakistan's intervention at the debate, Harish accused Islamabad of "misusing" the forum to "peddle its false narrative.""It was not our intention to bring other issues in this forum, but my delegation is compelled to respond to Pakistan, as it has chosen to misuse today's discussion to peddle its false narrative," the Indian envoy said."For the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir, it has always been, is, and will remain an integral and inalienable part of India. This is the constitutional and legal reality that Pakistan deliberately chooses to ignore."Harish also asserted that Pakistan's illegal occupation of Indian territory remained the only unresolved issue concerning Jammu and Kashmir."The only outstanding issue with regard to Jammu and Kashmir is the naked aggression and illegal occupation of India's sovereign territory by Pakistan."On the Indus Water Treaty , the Indian envoy reiterated New Delhi's longstanding position, saying Pakistan's support for crossborder terrorism had eroded the trust necessary for bilateral cooperation."Our position on the Indus Water Treaty remains clear and consistent. Cooperation on the basis of mutual trust and goodwill cannot be expected when crossborder terrorism is regularly deployed as an instrument of State policy."In his concluding statement at UN, Harish urged Pakistan to focus on addressing its own domestic challenges instead of targeting India."Instead of pointing fingers at India, Pakistan would serve itself and its people far better by putting its own house in order."
Article Summary: India on wednesday once again hit out at pakistan over its continued use of crossborder terrorism. New delhi also reiterated that jammu and kashmir "has always been, is, and will remain" an integral and inalienable part of india. Remarks were made by india's permanent representative to the united nations, ambassador parvathaneni harish.

Article Title: 'Don't waste time': SC nixes plea against police lathicharge on Jantar Mantar protesters
Article Text: NEW DELHI: A Supreme Court bench of CJI Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and V Mohana Wednesday declined to accept an advocate's suggestion that it take suo motu cognisance of the police action against protesters at Jantar Mantar.The bench said the advocate shouldn't waste his own as well as judicial time on issues not before the court. When the advocate said he had video footage of the violence and police lathicharge, and would file it for viewing by the court, the bench replied, "We do not want to watch videos during judicial work."Meanwhile, Supreme Court Bar Association led by senior advocate Vikas Singh passed a resolution condemning the “brutal lathicharge on innocent students”.“The use of excessive and disproportionate force against peaceful students and members of bar is deeply disturbing and wholly unacceptable in a democratic society,” said the resolution, passed 600 members of SCBA, without mentioning stone pelting and damage to public property by protesters.It sought an “immediate, impartial and time-bound inquiry into the incident, identify those responsible for excessive use of force” for appropriate action in accordance with law and measures to prevent recurrence of such incidents.
Article Summary: The bench said the advocate shouldn't waste his own as well as judicial time on issues not before the court. Supreme court bar association led by senior advocate vikas singh passed a resolution condemning the “brutal lathicharge on innocent students”

Article Title: EAM talks of trade with Rubio, sailors with Sergey Lavrov
Article Text:
Jaishankar meets US Secretary of State Marco Rubio

S Jaishankar had a meeting with his Marco Rubio on the sidelines of Asean in Manila, focusing on issues related to trade and tariffs along with the critical minerals partnership. According to a US readout, they agreed on the importance of finalising the interim trade deal, which is "almost complete". They also discussed finalising key defence agreements to fulfil commitments made between President Donald Trump and PM Narendra Modi. "Our relationship with India is vitally important," said Rubio on X.The US ambassador to India said Rubio may visit this year. Jaishankar said the meet focused on priority areas for India-US Comprehensive Global Strategic Partnership.In a meeting with his Russia counterpart, Sergey Lavrov, Jaishankar reiterated India's strong concerns about the safety of Indian seafarers. He said they also discussed trade, energy and science.
Article Summary: Jaishankar meets us secretary of state marco rubio on the sidelines of asean. They agreed on the importance of finalising the interim trade deal, which is "almost complete" they also discussed finalising key defence agreements to fulfil commitments made between president donald trump and pm narendra modi.

Article Title: Curbs on foreign funding not uniquely Indian, says govt, cites laws elsewhere ahead of bill to tweak FCRA
Article Text:
Govt cited comparable laws regulating foreign funding

NEW DELHI: Ahead of Parliament taking up the Foreign Contribution Regulation (Amendment) Bill, 2026, govt cited comparable laws regulating foreign funding, influence and lobbying in the US, UK, Australia and Canada to underline the "globally recognised" concern that "foreign money, when unregulated, can affect democratic institutions, electoral processes and public discourse".In a backgrounder on FCRA issued Wednesday, MHA said regulating foreign contributions and influence was "not a uniquely Indian approach", with leading democracies strengthening such frameworks over the past decade.The ministry cited the US Foreign Agents Registration Act (FARA), requiring registration and disclosure for those acting on behalf of a "foreign principal" in political activity or lobbying, and the UK's 2025 Foreign Influence Registration Scheme, which provides for up to two years' jail for political influence arrangements with a foreign power. It added that the EU has proposed transparency registers in member states for lobbying on behalf of non-EU govts.Rejecting the claim FCRA "bans" NGOs from receiving foreign funds, MHA said 16,200 associations were actively registered in 2024-25 and received Rs 22,963 crore in foreign contributions. It denied that FCRA targeted any religion or community, saying faith-based welfare activities remain eligible for foreign funding.On the proposed FCRA amendment allowing govt to seize assets of delisted NGOs, govt said the designated authority would manage assets created from foreign funds only after an organisation's FCRA registration lawfully ceases. Places of worship would retain their religious character, it said, while noting the provision for appeal.The ministry said restricting foreign nationals from key roles in FCRA-registered entities ensures foreign contributions are governed by persons with a verified connection to India. On mandatory Central approval for state-level FCRA investigations, it said FCRA is a central law."FCRA... ensures that foreign money enters India through a registered, accountable and disclosed channel... while enabling thousands of organisations to carry out legitimate, impactful work."
Article Summary: Foreign contribution regulation (amendment) bill, 2026 to be taken up in parliament. Govt cites comparable laws regulating foreign funding, influence and lobbying in us, uk, australia and canada. Government rejects claim fcra "bans" ngos from receiving foreign funds.

Article Title: Quad seeks free & open Indo-Pacific, pushes back against Chinese assertiveness
Article Text:
Jaishankar with Australian foreign minister Penny Wong, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Japan foreign minister Toshimitsu Motegi during Quad Foreign Ministers Meeting

India's policy of "multi-alignment" and strategic autonomy was evident in Manila Wednesday as external affairs minister S Jaishankar met his China counterpart Wang Yi in the Philippines and also joined a meeting of the Quad foreign ministers that had US secretary of state Marco Rubio declaring Quad remained a priority and will meet again later this year.The Quad meeting saw discussions on the situation in East China Sea and South China Sea, with Japan opposing any unilateral attempt to change the status quo by force or coercion and expressing grave concerns over export restrictions, including on critical minerals. The Quad meeting took place amid escalating China-Philippines tensions over another flare-up in the South China Sea.Even in the absence of a summit, Quad comprising Australia, India, Japan and the US has remained functional with the foreign ministers meeting for the second time this year, after they met in Delhi in May. Jaishankar, Rubio and their Australia and Japanese counterparts, Penny Wong and Toshimitsu Motegi respectively, met on the margins of the ASEAN-related meetings in Manila.In a joint press release, they reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening a free and open Indo-Pacific and their “unwavering” support for ASEAN unity and centrality, while also declaring they were united in their conviction that peace and stability in the Indo-Pacific maritime domain underpin the security and prosperity of the region.“We are deeply invested in the region’s success and in cooperating with ASEAN and its member states,” said the release. The statement outlined shared priorities related to maritime security and economic security, critical and emerging technologies, and humanitarian assistance in support for the implementation of the ASEAN Outlook on the Indo-Pacific (AOIP), which envisages ASEAN Centrality as the underlying principle for promoting cooperation in the Indo-Pacific.According to a Japanese readout, the ministers looked forward to the convening of the next Quad Leaders’ Summit. However, there was no word on the dates for the summit that India has tried unsuccessfully to host so far. In fact, unlike the joint statement issued after the meeting in Delhi, the joint press release made no mention of the summit. With President Donald Trump unlikely to visit India this year, the summit now is expected to take place only on the margins of the G20 summit in the US in December. The foreign ministers are likely to meet again though on the sidelines of a G20 ministerial in Atlanta in October.“The United States, India, Australia, and Japan share a vision of a free and open Indo-Pacific that is grounded in the rule of law, sovereignty, and territorial integrity,” said Rubio in a post on X, adding that strong cooperation is vital to supporting ASEAN’s own priority and centrality in the region.US ambassador Sergio Gor, who joined Rubio for the Quad meeting, said tere was a very productive discussion on delivering results related to a free and open Indo-Pacific. “Our four nations share a common vision,” he said.
Article Summary: India's policy of "multi-alignment" and strategic autonomy was evident in manila wednesday. External affairs minister s jaishankar met his china counterpart wang yi in the philippines. He also joined a meeting of the quad foreign ministers that had us secretary of state marco rubio declaring quad remained a priority.

Article Title: Jaishankar flags need for fair trade, supply chain reliability in meeting with Wang Yi
Article Text:
Jaishankar with Chinese counterpart Wang Yi

External affairs minister S Jaishankar underlined the significance of fair market access, trade balance and supply chain reliability in a meeting with his Chinese counterpart Wang Yi, while welcoming recent measures to normalise Sino-Indian ties like the upgrading of the visa framework and resumption of direct flights, Kailash pilgrimage and border trade.In a meeting in Manila on the margins of ASEAN events, Jaishankar also stressed that border peace and tranquility will remain a prerequisite for normal ties. Ahead of the BRICS summit India will host in September, he thanked Wang for China's support for India's presidency. While a final confirmation from Beijing is still awaited, President Xi is likely to attend the summit.India's relations with China have improved considerably since PM Narendra Modi and Xi met in Kazan in 2024, marking the end of the border standoff in eastern Ladakh. However, for India, addressing economic friction in ties including over the asymmetrical $ 100 billion trade imbalance and the need for Indian manufacturing to reliably access Chinese raw materials remains an essential next step for broader normalisation.According to official sources, India continues to raise issues related to trade restrictions imposed by Beijing and is working with the Chinese government to ensure predictability in supply chains for trade consistent with international practices."Fair market access and a trade balance ranks high in that regard. There are also concerns about predictability of supply chains. Facilitating both official and people-to-people exchanges merit our attention. We also need to agree on the meetings of various mechanisms and platforms as per our mutual priorities," said the minister in his opening remarks, underscoring the significance of the meeting in an extremely complicated global situation.Jaishankar said that while both sides had engaged to ensure border peace since October 2024, this issue would continue to need constant attention and that relevant mechanisms in that domain had to be given full support and strong encouragement.“It is natural that as two large and important nations, and that too proximate neighbours, India and China would have their own particular interests. That is why our leaders had agreed that differences should not become disputes. It is the responsibility of diplomacy to properly manage them,” said the minister.The minister reiterated India's position that the bilateral relationship must be built around the "3 mutuals" or mutual respect, mutual interest and mutual sensitivity."We believe that a stable and cooperative relationship can best be developed on the basis of mutual respect, mutual interest and mutual sensitivity," said Jaishankar, adding that such a relationship can make a valuable contribution to a multi-polar Asia and a multi-polar world.The minister said that relations have been gradually normalising since the October 2024 Kazan Modi-Xi meeting and this was further affirmed when the leaders met in Tianjin last year. Modi travelled to Tianjin in August 2024 for the SCO summit.
Article Summary: S jaishankar underlined the significance of fair market access, trade balance and supply chain reliability in a meeting with his chinese counterpart wang yi. He also stressed that border peace and tranquility will remain a prerequisite for normal ties. The minister said that relations have been gradually normalising since the october 2024 kazan modi-xi meeting.

Article Title: 'Unacceptable in a democratic society': SC bar bodies condemn police action against protesters, lawyers during CJP march
Article Text:
Delhi Police clashed with protesters during the CJP's Parliament march on July 20

NEW DELHI: Two Supreme Court bar bodies on Wednesday condemned the alleged police brutality against students and lawyers during the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP)-led July 20 march to Parliament, calling for an impartial inquiry into the "excessive use of force" and demanding accountability from those responsible.The Supreme Court Bar Association (SCBA), headed by its president and senior advocate Vikas Singh, in a resolution, alleged that the police action left several students and members of the legal fraternity, including SCBA members, injured.The resolution, issued by SCBA secretary Pragya Baghel, described the use of force against the protesters as "deeply disturbing and wholly unacceptable in a democratic society".The bar body urged the authorities concerned to conduct an immediate, impartial and time-bound inquiry into the incident, identify those responsible for the alleged excessive use of force, and take appropriate action in accordance with the law.It also called for proper medical treatment and assistance for all injured students and members of the legal fraternity, while urging authorities to take steps to prevent similar incidents in the future."The association stands in solidarity with all those injured and reiterates its commitment to protecting constitutional rights, human dignity and the rule of law," the resolution added.In a separate statement, the Supreme Court Advocates-on-Record Association (SCAORA) expressed deep concern over "reports and visuals showing the use of force by Delhi Police against students and young protesters" who had gathered to peacefully raise concerns over the integrity of public examinations and the future of the country's youth.SCAORA, in its resolution, maintained that peaceful dissent was not an act of defiance but an exercise of fundamental freedoms guaranteed under Articles 19(1)(a) and 19(1)(b) of the Constitution. It added that while the State has the authority to maintain public order, any restriction on these freedoms must meet the constitutional requirements of legality, necessity and proportionality.The association called for an independent and impartial examination of allegations of excessive force, immediate medical assistance for those injured, and a more restrained, dialogue-based approach while engaging with peaceful protesters."The strength of our Republic lies not in suppressing dissent but in protecting it within the framework of the Constitution. The true measure of constitutional governance is not how the State responds to agreement, but how it responds to peaceful disagreement," the resolution added.On Monday, thousands of students demanding the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the NEET paper leak issue and alleged irregularities in the examination system attempted to march to Parliament but were stopped by police using tear gas and batons.
Article Summary: Two supreme court bar bodies have condemned the alleged police brutality against students and lawyers. The supreme court bar association (scba) alleged that the police action left several students and members of the legal fraternity, including scba members, injured.

Article Title: Evening news wrap: Sonam Wangchuk sets condition for ending fast; Rahul Gandhi demands Dharmendra Pradhan's exit over NEET row & more
Article Text:
File photo: Activist Sonam Wangchuk and Congress MP Rahul Gandhi

Sonam Wangchuk to end fast only if protesters get legal immunity.

to end fast only if protesters get legal immunity. Rahul Gandhi demands Dharmendra Pradhan 's resignation over NEET row.

demands 's resignation over NEET row. Kalyan Banerjee suspended, calls rebel NCPI MPs "traitors".

Sensex, Nifty tumble as Iran tensions and Trump tariffs rattle markets.

US-Iran tensions deepen with fresh strikes, warnings and regional alerts.

Here are the top stories of the day:

Sonam Wangchuk says he'll end fast only after assurance for protesters

Rahul Gandhi demands Dharmendra Pradhan's exit over NEET row

Kalyan Banerjee suspended, calls rebel MPs 'traitors'

Sensex, Nifty slide as global risks spook markets

US-Iran tensions escalate amid strikes, regional alerts

Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk has refused to end his hunger strike over the NEET paper leak until the Centre guarantees that no legal action will be taken against participants of the July 20 'Chalo Sansad' march. In a letter to Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh, Wangchuk said he wanted to return to teaching but would continue his fast unless the government assured protection for peaceful protesters and addressed key demands on accountability. Read full story Congress leader Rahul Gandhi accused the Centre of turning India's education system into a "rigged system" over the alleged NEET-UG paper leak and demanded education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. Backing student protests, he alleged 152 paper leaks in the last decade with "zero convictions" and criticised police action against demonstrators.TMC MP Kalyan Banerjee was suspended from the Lok Sabha for the remainder of the Monsoon Session over alleged indecent and misogynistic remarks against women MPs during a heated exchange with rebel NCPI members. After the suspension, Banerjee called the rebel MPs "traitors" and claimed they lacked public support in West Bengal.Equity markets fell sharply, with the Sensex shedding 715 points and the Nifty slipping below 24,000 as escalating US-Iran tensions, surging crude oil prices and fresh tariff threats from US President Donald Trump dampened investor sentiment. Pharma stocks led the losses after Trump's proposed tariffs on imported generic medicines, while a weaker rupee and rising US Treasury yields added to the market pressure, wiping out nearly Rs 4.25 lakh crore in investor wealth.The US and Iran exchanged fresh warnings as secretary of state Marco Rubio said Washington remained open to diplomacy but would not tolerate threats to commercial shipping or the Strait of Hormuz. Iran claimed to have targeted military bases in Jordan, while reports also emerged of explosions in southern Iran and an alleged US strike on Larak Island. Bahrain and Saudi Arabia activated emergency alerts as regional tensions continued to intensify.
Article Summary: Sensex, nifty tumble as iran tensions and trump tariffs rattle markets. Us-iran tensions deepen with fresh strikes, warnings and regional alerts. Rahul gandhi demands dharmendra pradhan's resignation over neet row. Kalyan banerjee suspended, calls rebel ncpi mps "traitors"

Article Title: India-China ties gradually normalising since Modi-Xi Kazan meeting: Jaishankar
Article Text:
Jaishankar with Chinesecounterpart Wang Yi

NEW DELHI: External affairs minister S Jaishankar on Wednesday said India-China ties have been steadily improving since Prime Minister Narendra Modi and President Xi Jinping met in Russia's Kazan in October 2024. He also stressed that peace and tranquility along the border remain the "pre-requisite" for normal bilateral relations.Speaking to Chinese foreign minister Wang Yi in Manila on the sidelines of the Asean foreign ministers' meeting, Jaishankar also raised India's concerns over market access, trade imbalance and the predictability of supply chains as New Delhi and Beijing continue efforts to normalise ties after the prolonged eastern Ladakh military standoff, PTI reported."Since the meeting of our leaders in Kazan in October 2024, relations between India and China have been gradually normalising. This direction was further affirmed when they met in Tianjin last August," Jaishankar said.He said India believes that a stable relationship between the two neighbours must be based on "mutual respect, mutual interest and mutual sensitivity.""We believe that a stable and cooperative relationship can best be developed on the basis of mutual respect, mutual interest and mutual sensitivity.Such a relationship can make a valuable contribution to a multi-polar Asia and a multi-polar world," he added.Underscoring the importance of stability along the Line of Actual Control (LAC), Jaishankar said normal ties cannot progress without peace in the border areas."Peace and tranquility in the border areas is obviously the pre-requisite for normal ties. Since October 2024, both sides have engaged to ensure that important objective," he said."This will continue to need our constant attention. Relevant mechanisms in this domain must be given full support and strong encouragement," he added.Jaishankar also reiterated that differences between the two countries should not be allowed to turn into disputes."It is natural that as two large and important nations, and that too proximate neighbours, India and China would have their own particular interests. That is why our leaders had agreed that differences should not become disputes. It is the responsibility of diplomacy to properly manage them," he said.The external affairs minister said India expects progress on long-pending economic issues, including fair market access and trade."Fair market access and a trade balance ranks high in that regard. There are also concerns about predictability of supply chains. Facilitating both official and people-to-people exchanges merit our attention," Jaishankar said."We also need to agree on the meetings of various mechanisms and platforms as per our mutual priorities," he added.Jaishankar welcomed the confidence-building measures taken over the past several months, including the resumption of direct flights, the easing of visa rules, the restart of the Kailash Manasarovar Yatra and the reopening of border trade.He also thanked China for supporting India's initiatives during its Brics chairmanship. "India is the chair of the Brics this year. Let me take the opportunity to appreciate the support that China has given to various Brics activities and initiatives during our chairship," he said.
Article Summary: S jaishankar said india-china ties have been steadily improving since pm modi and president xi jinping met in russia's kazan in october 2024. He stressed that peace and tranquility along the border remain the "pre-requisite" for normal bilateral relations.

Article Title: Dinoblade release date and timings for all regions
Article Text:
Dinoblade officially releases on July 23 (Image via Team Spino)

Dinoblade release date

Dinoblade release timings for all regions

Americas

Pacific Time (PT): July 23, 2026, at 10:00 AM

Mountain Time (MT): July 23, 2026, at 11:00 AM

Central Time (CT): July 23, 2026, at 12:00 PM

Eastern Standard Time (EST): July 23, 2026, at 1:00 PM

Europe

British Standard Time (BST): July 23, 2026, at 6:00 PM

Central European Summer Time (CEST): July 23, 2026, at 7:00 PM

Moscow Standard Time (MSK): July 23, 2026, at 8:00 PM

Asia

India Standard Time (IST): July 23, 2026, at 10:30 PM

China Standard Time (CST): July 24, 2026, at 1:00 AM

Japan Standard Time (JST): July 24, 2026, at 2:00 AM

Oceania

Australian Eastern Standard Time (AEST): July 24, 2026, at 3:00 AM

New Zealand Standard Time (NZST): July 24, 2026, at 5:00 AM

What is Dinoblade?

Will Dinoblade be available on consoles?

One of the most exciting indie game launches of the summer is almost here. Dinoblade, the pre-historic soulslike that has been building a passionate following since its announcement, is officially launching on July 23, 2026, and the global release timings have now been confirmed.It is set to be one of the most unique releases of recent times, with fans excited to see what the developer shave in store for them. In the section below, we take a detailed look at when Dinoblade goes live across the world.Dinoblade officially releases on July 23, 2026. The game launches simultaneously across the world at 10 AM PT, meaning players will be able to access it at the same time.Based on the regional time differences, most regions will have access on July 23, while players in China, Japan, Australia, and New Zealand will see the game unlock in the early hours of July 24 local time.Based on the 10 AM PT launch timings, here is the complete regional breakdown of when Dinoblade goes live:Accordingly, you will be able to download and play the game at the aforementioned timings.Dinoblade is a new Souslike game that combines intense combat with that of prehistoric creature design. You are essentially in control of a powerful blade-wielding dinosaur who must battle its way through a world overrun by enemies.The game’s innovative combat system and art direction have been compared to some of the most popular titles of the last decade. It has been one of the most discussed upcoming indie releases of 2026, with fans excited to play as a dinosaur and slaughter the enemies.The highly anticipated Dinoblade is set to be a PC exclusive title at the time of launch. Given that it is an indie game, the developers at Team Spino have prioritized the launch on PC, as it will reach the maximum number of users.Based on the reception from the community, we might possibly see Dinoblade releasing on consoles in the future. However, for now, the game is set to be exclusively available on PC, and you will be able to download it through Steam.
Article Summary: Dinoblade is a new souslike game that combines intense combat with that of prehistoric creature design. The game launches simultaneously across the world at 10 am pt, meaning players will be able to access it at the same time.

Article Title: US strikes Iran for 12th night, targets missile, drone and air defence sites
Article Text: The US military launched another round of strikes against Iran on Wednesday, marking the 12th consecutive night of attacks, US Central Command (CENTCOM) said. The strikes targeted Iranian military sites, including maritime capabilities, missile and drone storage facilities, coastal surveillance sites and air defence assets.In a post on X, Centcom added that US forces had struck dozens of Iranian locations on land while resuming a blockade against Iran at sea.“At 10:30 p.m. ET on July 22, U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) forces completed another round of strikes against Iran for the 12th consecutive night,” the post read, adding "U.S. forces struck Iranian military targets including maritime capabilities, missile and drone storage facilities, coastal surveillance sites, and air defense assets."The command has maintained that the strikes are aimed at reducing Iran’s ability to target shipping in regional waters. The US military said the latest operation lasted five hours.“This month, American forces have targeted dozens of Iranian military sites on land while resuming a blockade against Iran at sea,” CENTCOM said, adding that nine commercial vessels had been redirected and one disabled to prevent ships from entering or departing Iranian ports.The post further added that 50,000 US service members were operating across the Middle East. “More than 50,000 U.S. service members are operating across the Middle East and remain highly vigilant, focused, lethal, and ready,” it said.The latest strikes came as US president Donald Trump said Iran was “getting hit so hard” and described the conflict as a “skirmish”. Speaking in Georgia on Wednesday, Trump claimed Tehran wanted to “make a deal” but was not ready for an agreement.“This skirmish that we have with the Islamic Republic of Iran… They're getting hit so hard, and they want to make a deal,” Trump said. Meanwhile, Iranian foreign minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi warned that any attack on Iran, including its infrastructure, would face a “decisive response”, while other officials also vowed “decisive action”.
Article Summary: Us central command (centcom) said the latest operation lasted five hours. The strikes targeted iranian military sites, including maritime capabilities, missile and drone storage facilities, coastal surveillance sites and air defence assets. The command has maintained that the strikes are aimed at reducing iran’s ability to target shipping in regional waters.

Article Title: Indian trucker with legal California driver's licence arrested over $3 million stolen powder
Article Text:
Indian trucker Deepak Kumar. (Photo: Greenfield Police Dept)

Truck tracked using camera system

California licensing policy draws criticism

An Indian national is in the custody of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) after authorities in Pennsylvania accused him of transporting nearly $3 million worth of stolen tungsten oxide powder, The New York Post reported.The suspect, identified as Deepak Kumar , 31, of Fresno, California, was arrested after investigators tracked a truck carrying 10 pallets of the industrial material, which authorities said belonged to Mitsubishi Materials Corporation.The shipment was scheduled to be picked up on June 24, but was delayed by a day. Company officials alerted police after they were unable to contact the driver on June 26.According to The New York Post, investigators located the truck the following day with the help of a Flock camera system.According to court records, Kumar allegedly presented officers with three forms of identification — an invalid work visa, a fraudulent New York state driver's licence and a valid California driver's licence.He was arrested on theft-related charges before being transferred to McFarland, California, where he remains in ICE custody.Authorities said the stolen tungsten oxide powder was valued at $2,857,500. The material is used in products including armour-piercing ammunition, ceramics, lasers, optical equipment and high-tech window coatings.The arrest has renewed scrutiny of California's policy allowing individuals with federally issued Employment Authorization Documents (EADs) to obtain commercial driver's licences.According to the New York Post, critics argue that the policy has created a loophole by enabling some undocumented immigrants with temporary federal work authorisation to legally obtain commercial driving licences.Supporters of the policy, however, have maintained that eligibility is based on valid federal employment authorisation rather than immigration status alone.
Article Summary: Deepak kumar, 31, of fresno, california, was arrested after investigators tracked a truck carrying 10 pallets of the industrial material. The shipment was scheduled to be picked up on june 24, but was delayed by a day. Authorities said the stolen tungsten oxide powder was valued at $2,857,500.

Article Title: Indian Navy commissions INS Malvan, the second of Mahe-class Anti-Submarine Warfare Shallow Water Craft
Article Text:
INS Malvan

INS Malvan

About INS Malvan

The Indian Navy on Wednesday commissioned INS Malvan, the second Mahe-class Anti-Submarine Warfare Shallow Water Craft (ASW-SWC), at the Karwar naval Base in Karnataka.Built by Cochin Shipyard Limited with over 80% indigenous content, is part of the Navy's eight-vessel Mahe-class programme aimed at enhancing shallow-water surveillance and underwater defence.The commissioning ceremony was presided over by Air Chief Marshal AP Singh, chief of the air staff, in the presence of Vice Admiral Sanjay Vatsayan, flag officer commanding-in-chief of the western naval command.Senior naval officers, representatives of Cochin Shipyard Limited and veterans also attended the event, marking the induction of the first Indian naval warship to be commissioned at Karwar.Addressing the ceremony, Air Chief Marshal AP Singh underlined the importance of maritime security in safeguarding global trade and national prosperity."We all understand how maritime security is important for any nation, as maritime routes decide trade and trade decides its destiny. Indian Ocean region is important to us. Indian Navy is playing a vital role in ensuring secure sea lines of communication and trade continues unhindered. With 80% indigenous content in this warship, we will soon realise the vision of Atmanirbhar Bharat. Indian Navy, right from the beginning, adopted this mantra, even though we (IAF) started very late on this track as various sectors take different speeds of time to grow.Vice Admiral Sanjay Vatsayan described the commissioning of INS Malvan as another milestone in India's maritime journey and said the warship's more than 80% indigenous content reflected the country's growing self-reliance in defence manufacturing.He noted that INS Malvan is the first Indian naval ship to be commissioned at Karwar and the seventh warship inducted into the Navy this year, with another 12 vessels expected to join the fleet.Designed for operations in coastal and shallow waters, the class is intended to detect, track and engage enemy submarines while also undertaking coastal surveillance, search and rescue, and low-intensity maritime security operations.INS Malvan hasa displacement between 896 and 1,100 tonnes and length of 78 metres. The ships can achieve a maximum speed of 25 knots (45 km) and have a range of 1,800 nautical miles (3,300 km) at a speed of 14 knots (25 km), with an endurance of 14 days.They are propelled by three marine diesel engines producing 6 MW of power, each coupled with a water jet, making them the largest Indian Navy warships to use water-jet propulsion.Waterjet propulsion is one of the key features of INS Malvan. Instead of using conventional propellers, the system draws in water from beneath the hull and expels it at high speed through nozzles to propel the ship.This gives the warship better manoeuvrability, quicker acceleration and improved performance in shallow coastal waters. It also produces less underwater noise and reduces the risk of damage from underwater obstacles, making it well suited for anti-submarine warfare operations.The weapons suite of the Mahe-class reflects its primary mission. It includes a forward-mounted RBU-6000 rocket launcher, triple 324 mm lightweight torpedo tubes on both port and starboard sides armed with Advanced Light-Weight Torpedoes, and anti-submarine mine-laying rails.For defensive measures, the ships are fitted with Mahindra Defence Systems-supplied torpedo decoy launching systems as part of the Integrated Anti-Submarine Warfare Defence Suite. Surface warfare capability is provided by a 30 mm naval gun and two 12.7 mm stabilized remote-controlled guns, the Indian variant of Elbit Systems’ Remote Controlled Naval Weapon Station. This mix of offensive and defensive systems ensures the vessels can operate independently in contested littoral zones.
Article Summary: Ins malvan is part of the navy's eight-vessel mahe-class programme aimed at enhancing shallow-water surveillance and underwater defence. Built by cochin shipyard limited with over 80% indigenous content, it is the first indian naval ship to be commissioned at karwar.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Lao Tzu: 'Time is a created thing. To say ‘I don’t have time,’ is like saying, ‘I don’t want to' and a reality check about priorities
Article Text:
Quote of the day by Lao Tzu

Nothing is rushed in nature

Timeless quote on time

Lao Tzu (also written as Laozi) was an ancient Chinese philosopher and writer, traditionally considered the founder of Taoism (or Daoism). He is believed to have lived around the 6th century BCE, roughly around the same time as Confucius. Legend says he worked as a scholar and keeper of the royal archives for the Zhou dynasty before growing weary of society's corruption, riding off into the west on a water buffalo, and writing the Tao Te Ching, the central text of Taoist thought, before disappearing.Whether Lao Tzu was a single historical person or a title representing a group of wise thinkers, his influence on Eastern philosophy is massive. Taoism focuses on living in harmony with the Tao (the "Way"), which is the natural, flowing order of the universe. Rather than forcing things, fighting against reality, or chasing endless desires, Taoism preaches simplicity, humility, and effortlessly moving with life's natural rhythm."Time is a created thing. To say ‘I don’t have time,’ is like saying, ‘I don’t want to" is one of the most memorable quotes of Lao Tzu because it is ageless. It teaches us something that we encounter every day -- someone giving the excuse of little time and, in fact, clarifying their priority.In modern Westernized culture, we tend to view time as a ticking clock, a rigid, scarce resource that is constantly running out. We divide our days into minutes, set strict schedules, and measure our worth by how productive we are.Lao Tzu and the early Taoists saw the world very differently. In nature, nothing is rushed, yet everything gets done, he said. Seasons change on their own, trees grow without straining, and rivers flow around obstacles without forcing their way through.To a Taoist, nature does not operate on a clock; it operates on presence and priority. When humans invented clocks, calendars, and rigid daily routines, we created the artificial concept of "not having enough time." Lao Tzu’s philosophy reminds us that time isn't a physical monster chasing us: it's a mental construct shaped entirely by what we choose to care about.This quote is a blunt, radical reality check about human agency, responsibility, and priorities.Lao Tzu said time as we understand the concept in not an unyielding physical constraint like gravity. Time is made up of choices. Everyone on Earth gets the same 24 hours in a day. The fabric of a day expands or shrinks based on how we choose to fill it. When you say, "I wish I had time to exercise," or "I don't have time to read," you are acting as if time is an external boss ruling over you. In reality, you are the creator of your schedule. You "create" time for the things you value, and you destroy time for the things you ignore.Not having time for something is a lie. If you don't have time for something, it means you don't want to do that. Lao Tzu strips away our excuses. He points out that saying "I don't have time" is almost always a passive-aggressive translation for:"This isn't a high priority for me right now.""I don't care enough about this to make room for it.""I am unwilling to sacrifice something else to do this."It forces us to own our decisions. If you truly wanted to do something, you would drop something less important to make room for it.While the quote sounds harsh at first, its deeper purpose is actually liberating. If time were truly a rigid cage beyond your control, you would be a helpless victim of your busy life. But if time is something you create, then you hold the keys. You are in complete control of your priorities. Realizing this allows you to stop feeling guilty about the things you don't do. Instead of saying, "I don't have time to learn a language," you can honestly say, "I'm choosing to focus my energy on my career and family right now, so learning a language isn't my priority. " That simple shift restores your personal agency.
Article Summary: Lao tzu (also written as laozi) was an ancient chinese philosopher and writer. He is traditionally considered the founder of taoism (or daoism) he is believed to have lived around the 6th century bce, roughly around the same time as confucius. His influence on eastern philosophy is massive.

Article Title: Who is Amish Shah? Indian-origin doctor wins Arizona democratic primary
Article Text:
Amish Shah wins key democratic primary

Who is Amish Shah?



Indian-origin former Arizona state lawmaker Amish Shah won the Democratic primary for Arizona's 1st Congressional District on Wednesday.He will now face Republican Jay Feely in the November general election in a closely watched race that could help determine control of the US House of Representatives.Shah's victory over Republican-turned-Democrat Marlene Galán-Woods marks another setback for the national Democratic Party. The Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee (DCCC) has faced scrutiny after its preferred candidates were also defeated in primaries in Maine's 2nd Congressional District and California's 22nd Congressional District.Arizona's 1st Congressional District, which includes Scottsdale and surrounding northeastern suburbs, is considered one of the most competitive House battlegrounds in the country.The seat is currently held by Republican David Schweikert, who chose to run for governor instead of seeking re-election but lost the GOP primary on Tuesday to Representative Andy Biggs. Schweikert has represented the district since 2023, after previously serving Arizona's 5th and 6th Congressional districts.Amish Shah, a physician and former Arizona state lawmaker, was born and raised in Chicago to parents who immigrated from India in the 1960s while pursuing engineering studies in the United States.Shah earned a bachelor's degree in economics from Northwestern University, before completing his medical degree through the university's Honors Program in Medical Education. He later worked at international strategy consulting firm LEK Consulting and went on to earn a Master of Public Health from the University of California, Berkeley, on a full merit scholarship.He completed his residency in emergency medicine at one of New York City's busiest Level I trauma centres and won the American Academy of Emergency Medicine's National Resident Research Competition for developing a new method of treating critically ill patients.Shah served as a faculty member and academic researcher at Mount Sinai Medical Center in New York, where he taught medical students and residents and published research in peer-reviewed journals. He also led the New York Jets' Airway Management Team, helping strengthen the NFL's emergency response protocols through research on managing catastrophic injuries, which he later presented to the NFL Physicians' Society.Shah completed a sports medicine fellowship at the University of Arizona, where his clinical work and research focused on ultrasound-guided diagnostics and procedures. He is board-certified in emergency medicine and sports medicine and currently practises across Arizona, treating both adults and children.Beyond medicine, Shah founded the Arizona Vegetarian Food Festival, a philanthropic initiative aimed at promoting healthy eating and preventing lifestyle-related diseases.Outside his medical practice, Shah enjoys playing basketball, flying small aircraft and adventure travel. He is also fluent in Gujarati and speaks conversational Spanish.
Article Summary: Amish shah, a physician and former arizona state lawmaker, was born and raised in chicago to parents who immigrated from india in the 1960s. He will now face republican jay feely in the november general election in a closely watched race that could help determine control of the us house of representatives.

Article Title: Trump considers military strikes in Mali, US plans to target al-Qaida-linked JNIM
Article Text:
Trump administration weighs action in Mali (ANI image on left)

JNIM ambush kills dozens of soldiers

The Trump administration is considering military strikes in Mali targeting Jama'at Nasr al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM), an al-Qaida-linked militant group, according to current and former US officials cited by The Washington Post.The proposed operation comes amid escalating violence in northern Mali, where JNIM and the separatist Azawad Liberation Front (FLA) have intensified attacks against the Malian military in a bid to seize territory. Since early July, the two groups have been battling government forces despite pursuing different long-term objectives.If approved, Mali would become the eighth country where President Donald Trump has authorised military action since returning to office.The latest escalation follows a JNIM ambush on a Malian military convoy in northern Mali, reportedly leaving at least 50 soldiers dead or captured. The militant group also released videos purportedly showing Malian troops surrendering. Among those reportedly killed or captured were Russian fighters from Russia's Africa Corps, which replaced the Wagner Group in supporting Mali's military.Mali's military said it retaliated with airstrikes that killed around 20 JNIM fighters. The ambush took place between the northern cities of Gao and Anefis, an area where JNIM has been attempting to expand its control.The worsening security situation has raised concerns about the stability of Mali's military-led government, which came to power through coups in 2020 and 2021.The crisis deepened in April when Mali's defence minister was killed in a coordinated attack by JNIM and the FLA. While the FLA seeks an independent homeland in northern Mali, JNIM aims to impose Islamist rule across the country, making their alliance one of tactical convenience rather than shared ideology.
Article Summary: Trump administration weighs action in mali. Jnim ambush on malian military convoy reportedly left at least 50 soldiers dead or captured. Among those reportedly killed or captured were russian fighters from russia's africa corps, which replaced the wagner group.

Article Title: Japan sizzles in first 'brutally hot' days of year
Article Text:
Japan swelters in heat

TOKYO: Japan sizzled under sweltering, life-threatening heat Wednesday, with temperatures expected to top 40Celsius in some cities in what govt has newly designated as a "brutally hot day". Authorities urged residents to use air conditioning liberally, hydrate regularly and avoid spending long hours outdoors.Japan has experienced increasingly severe summers in recent years, with the country recording its all-time high temperature of 41.8C last year. Even before this week's extreme heat, 4,580 people were taken to hospitals between July 6 and 12, including seven who later succumbed, according to the data available from the fire and disaster management agency.(This is a Reuters story)
Article Summary: Japan swelters under sweltering, life-threatening heat wednesday. Authorities urged residents to use air conditioning liberally, hydrate regularly and avoid spending long hours outdoors. Even before this week's extreme heat, 4,580 people were taken to hospitals between july 6 and 12.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Laozi: 'A good traveler has no fixed plans and is not intent on arriving' and how the best parts of life happen on the detours you never planned
Article Text:
'A good traveler has no fixed plans and is not intent on arriving'

A good traveler has no fixed plans and is not intent on arriving."

Ancient roots and modern meanings

Daodejing

Shan xing wu zhe ji

"A good traveler leaves no tracks or wheel ruts".

Daodejing

The freedom of focusing on the process

wu wei

Focusing on the Goal: Treating the present moment like a stepping stone, causing stress whenever unexpected delays happen.

Treating the present moment like a stepping stone, causing stress whenever unexpected delays happen. Focusing on the Process: Reacting smoothly to changing situations, treating unexpected surprises as useful moments rather than annoying failures.

Living in an unpredictable world

Picture a traveller standing at a busy intersection in a foreign city. Her phone battery dies, taking her maps, digital tickets, and step-by-step plans with it. Instead of panicking, she puts the phone in her pocket, turns down a side street lined with local bakeries, and spends the next three hours exploring an old neighbourhood absent from every guidebook. She has no schedule, no deadline, and no rush to reach a specific landmark.That shift in mindset captures the heart of the famous ancient saying: "The sentence points out a basic problem in how people live their lives. Most people plan their lives around specific destinations, whether a physical city, a job title, or a savings goal. By doing this, they turn the journey into a series of hurdles to clear as quickly as possible. The phrase suggests a totally different approach: treating life as an experience to enjoy directly rather than a set list of tasks to complete.The phrase "A good traveler has no fixed plans and is not intent on arriving" comes from Chapter 27 of the(Tao Te Ching), an ancient Chinese book written around the 4th century BCE.While tradition says the book was written by the wise thinker Laozi, the exact English words used today come from Stephen Mitchell's popular 1988 translation. Scholars of ancient Chinese point out that Mitchell’s version is a creative interpretation rather than a strict line-by-line translation. The original Chinese phrase,, translates more literally to:In ancient China, a traveller who left no tracks was a master of living in balance. They moved so naturally through a landscape that they caused no damage, left no heavy mark, and easily adapted to whatever terrain appeared. Mitchell translated that deep spiritual idea of smooth movement into everyday words, swapping the image of "leaving no tracks" for the idea of dropping strict plans.The historical background of theexplains why this idea mattered so much. Written during a time of constant war and political trouble in China, the book offered a direct challenge to the strict social rules and rigid government systems of the time. Laozi argued that trying to force the world into strict plans only causes stress and pain. True success, whether in ruling a country or living daily life, meant going with the natural flow of the world.At its heart, this idea relies on the concept of, often translated as "effortless action" or "going with the flow." It describes a mental state where a person reacts to real life as it actually happens, rather than trying to force events to fit a set plan.In modern psychology, this matches research on "flow states"—moments of deep focus where a person forgets about time and becomes fully wrapped up in what they are doing. When someone focuses only on a distant goal, their mind gets split. Part of their brain stays trapped in worry about the future, constantly checking how far they are from the finish line.Focusing only on the end result creates a common mental trap:By letting go of the need for a specific final result, a person stops treating today as a task to rush through to get to tomorrow.This ancient idea offers real value across modern life, especially in a world full of strict schedules and tight deadlines.In business and leadership, rigid long-term plans often fail when situations change fast. The most successful organizations work less like train drivers stuck on a single set of tracks and more like sailors adjusting their sails to changing winds. Modern business practices use this exact idea: keeping a clear general direction while remaining ready to drop old plans when real-world conditions change.In personal development and education, an obsession with fixed goals often leads to exhaustion and burnout. Students and workers who treat their careers as a single, inflexible path struggle when unexpected problems appear. Those who approach their work like flexible travellers treat surprising detours as chances to build new skills and find unexpected interests.Using this idea in daily life does not mean throwing away all goals or living in complete chaos. It simply means holding plans loosely. It means picking a direction while staying open to the path, knowing that the most meaningful experiences often happen on the unplanned detours.
Article Summary: "a good traveler has no fixed plans and is not intent on arriving" comes from chapter 27 of the(tao te ching), an ancient chinese book written around the 4th century bce. The phrase suggests a totally different approach: treating life as an experience to enjoy directly rather than a set list of tasks to complete.

Article Title: 'Ban Muslims from office': Laura Loomer calls Zohran Mamdani 'national security risk'
Article Text:
Zohran Mamdani and Laura Loomer (R)

America's far-right activist and Donald Trump loyalist Laura Loomer on Wednesday launched a scathing attack on New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani after he called Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu as a "war criminal" and said he was "not welcome in New York City."Loomer, who is Jewish, called for a ban on Muslims holding public office in the United States and urged President Donald Trump to reinstate a "Muslim ban.""Ban Muslims from holding office in America," Loomer said.She also appealed directly to MAGA supremo, writing: "We are never going to Make America Safe Again until President Trump goes back to implementing his Muslim ban. This time, make it a Muslim Ban as it was intended to be. Not an Islamic travel ban."Taking to X, Loomer accused Mamdani of sympathising with Iran and Hamas, and claimed, without providing evidence, that he posed a national security risk. "When you allow Muslims to participate in the American political system, you get Twelver Shia Iran regime sympathizers and Hamas lovers like Mamdani governing the largest city in America. It won’t end well."She further claimed that Mamdani would never receive FBI security clearance, saying, "I’m just glad the Trump administration confirmed to me that Mamdani will never be given an FBI clearance."Citing an unnamed source, Loomer wrote, "My senior FBI source told me, 'Senior FBI leadership has reason to believe Mamdani may be having direct communication with Islamic terrorist organizations. He is certainly a national security risk which is why we declined giving him an FBI approved security clearance. He won’t be getting one.'"Loomer added, "I pushed very hard for that to be the case even before Mamdani was elected," and further claimed, "Shocking my FBI source confirmed to me that they allegedly have reason to believe Mamdani is having direct communication with terror orgs."The remarks came after Mamdani intensified his criticism of Netanyahu in a video posted on X on Tuesday, saying the Israeli leader was "not welcome in New York City" and urging the US federal government to enforce the International Criminal Court's (ICC) arrest warrant against him.Calling Netanyahu "a war criminal," Mamdani accused him of committing atrocities during Israel's military campaign in Gaza."He is a war criminal, the architect of a horrific genocide against the Palestinian people," Mamdani said.He further alleged that Netanyahu was responsible "for the maiming of tens of thousands of children as those who survive undergo amputation without anaesthesia. For the targeting of neonatal hospitals and maternity care centres, denying newborns even the chance to live. For the countless people he starved as he blocked food and humanitarian aid from reaching them."Referring to the ICC arrest warrant, Mamdani said: "There is a reason the International Criminal Court issued a warrant for his arrest. As I've said, I agree with the ICC that Benjamin Netanyahu should be arrested and tried for his crimes as I do for anyone else charged by the ICC."
Article Summary: Far-right activist laura loomer has attacked new york city mayor zohran mamdani. She called for a ban on muslims holding public office in the united states and urged president donald trump to reinstate a "muslim ban" she also claimed, without providing evidence, that he posed a national security risk.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Che Guevara: 'The life of a single human being is worth millions of times more than all the property of the richest man on Earth' and how modern economic systems treat people like
Article Text:
'The life of a single human being is worth millions of times more than all the property of the richest man on earth'

"The life of a single human being is worth millions of times more than all the property of the richest man on earth".

The feeling that economic systems treat people like numbers on a spreadsheet.

A doctor’s speech in Havana

On Revolutionary Medicine

Placing people above property

uman beings have basic dignity that money cannot buy.

Focus on Property:

Focus on Life:

Real-world relevance today

Building a world that treats every human life as something beyond price.

When a single human life is weighed against material wealth, the right choice seems clear. Yet everyday real-world systems constantly do the math in reverse. Car companies weigh the cost of fixing a dangerous defect against the cost of paying out lawsuits for accidents. Investment firms buy up empty apartment buildings while unhoused people sleep on the streets outside. Modern society often measures value by what can be bought, sold, or protected behind gates.Against this financial way of thinking stands a bold statement:This quote rejects the idea of putting a price tag on people. It says that human life is so valuable that comparing it to money or property is a major moral mistake. The statement still matters today because it speaks to a common frustration:These words were spoken by Ernesto "Che" Guevara in Havana, Cuba, on August 19, 1960. Before he became a famous guerrilla leader in the Cuban Revolution, Guevara was a trained medical doctor.Guevara gave this speech, titled, to health workers and militia members. Cuba was undergoing massive political changes, and its healthcare system was struggling after hundreds of private doctors left the country.Guevara spoke to the remaining medical staff as a fellow doctor. He recalled his younger days traveling across South America as a medical student. On those trips, he saw poor families, untreated diseases, and children dying simply because their parents could not afford basic medicine.He explained how his experiences in the mountains during the revolution changed his thinking. Seeing poor farmworkers suffer from preventable illnesses taught him that doctoring could not just be about making money. He argued that a doctor’s real duty was to help build a society where no person's life was treated as worthless. The quote was not just an abstract idea; it was a clear instruction to health workers rebuilding a country's medical system.At its heart, the quote pushes a simple moral idea: HThe 18th-century philosopher Immanuel Kant famously wrote that everything in the world has either a price or a dignity. Things with a price can be replaced by money, but human beings have dignity, which cannot be replaced by anything.Guevara took this philosophical point and applied it to real-world politics and economics. The quote highlights two very different ways of viewing the world:Measures value through money, ownership, and land titles.Argues that human life and health carry an absolute value that no amount of physical property can equal.By placing one single life above "all the property of the richest man on earth," the quote sets up a clear rule. It insists that property rights are man-made laws meant to serve people. The moment property rules harm human health or survival, those rules lose their moral authority.The conflict between property rights and human survival remains a major issue across modern society.Look at global healthcare. During health crises, big drug companies closely guard their patents and formulas to protect their profits. Lower-income countries often face severe shortages of life-saving medicine because making cheaper versions is blocked by international trade laws. In these cases, financial ownership is given priority over human survival.Look also at housing in major cities. Local governments frequently deal with severe homelessness while thousands of investor-owned luxury apartments sit completely empty. When laws protect empty property assets while making it illegal for homeless people to sleep on public park benches, society acts as if property claims matter more than basic human shelter.The same principle applies to technology and corporate management. Social media companies often weigh user safety against corporate profit. They decide how much effort to spend on protecting users based on how it affects their earnings. When a platform chooses to design algorithms that keep people addicted at the expense of their mental health, it makes the exact choice Guevara spoke against.Putting this quote into practice means changing how institutions run. It requires companies, courts, and governments to make decisions where human safety and health always come first, no matter the financial cost.In 1960, Cuba used Guevara's ideas to build a free public healthcare system. Despite severe economic troubles and shortages, the nation prioritized free medical care and sent doctors to help during disasters around the world. It was a direct attempt to put these words into real practice.Whether in public health, city planning, or business, the core lesson remains unchanged:
Article Summary: Ernesto "che" guevara gave the quote in a speech in havana, cuba, in 1960. He argued that a doctor’s real duty was to help build a society where no person's life was treated as worthless. The conflict between property rights and human survival remains a major issue. In 1960, cuba used guevara's ideas to build a free public healthcare system.
